title: PACT v0 frame/Claim record
description: Re-authored from kernel transaction-record.schema.json (TRANSFER-PROVENANCE.md). required[] is the ONLY part the lenient runtime validator (record.js validateRecord) enforces; additionalProperties + patterns are the strict/documentary schema. A v0 record is a PACT frame (spec §2) carrying a Claim payload (spec §3).

type.description: frame type; v0=CLAIM, P2 adds CONTEST (caught defection) + VOUCH; P3 (spec §6) adds PREMISE (a human-owned assumption, creator-bound-on-read), CONFIRM (a cross-verify confirmation of a premise) + ACCEPT (a receiver-local REACH accept of a claim); U1 (plans/20) adds STAKE (a custody-signed root-bound presence/commitment, lock_expiry only — NO amount per creator-standing.js D5; magnitude is born at S6); U1 S4 (plans/23) adds SLASH (a crater-disciplined forfeiture: target_stake_id resolving to a real STAKE + a counterexample reason; the stake reads 'slashed' on >=2 distinct earned-standing slasher roots). NOTE: this enum is DOCUMENTARY — the lenient runtime validator (record.js validateRecord) enforces required[] only, never the enum.

payload.description: the Claim (spec §3); any JSON shape. P3 (spec §6) payload conventions, all enforced ON READ (the store is not a sandbox): PREMISE => {statement, scope, creator} (the self-asserted human creator counts ONLY when rootOf(src_persona_did)===creator); CONFIRM/CONTEST of a premise => {target_premise_id}; ACCEPT => {target_claim_id}. DISCRIMINANT (plans/08, ENFORCED type-blind in record.js validateRecord): a record carries AT MOST ONE of {target_claim_id, target_premise_id} — the present field selects the axis (target_claim_id => trust/direct claim-defection or ACCEPT/REACH; target_premise_id => grounding premise-contest). Both is the one cross-layer two-way path; a contest needing both axes MINTS TWO RECORDS.

target_premise_id.description: P3: the ATMS content-address (sha256 of the {statement,scope,creator} premise body via makePremise().id — NOT the frame record_id) that a CONFIRM/CONTEST targets (premise-level cross-verify / contest)

target_claim_id.description: the claim a CONTEST (P2) or an ACCEPT (P3 REACH) targets

creator.description: P3 PREMISE: the SELF-ASSERTED human_uid creator; counts only when rootOf(src_persona_did)===creator (creator-bound-on-read)

lock_expiry.description: U1 STAKE (plans/20): the commitment-window end (epoch ms). NO amount field — D5 (a self-asserted economic-cost is forgeable); the in-memory stake is a presence/lock marker, the magnitude is born at S6. SELF-ASSERTED with no clock validation in S1-S2 (a residual).

target_stake_id.description: U1 SLASH (plans/23): the content-addressed record_id of the STAKE being slashed. Counted ONLY when it resolves to a REAL verified STAKE of the slashed root (the F3-analog — a slash of a non-existent stake is IGNORED on read, closing pre-positioning).

reason.description: U1 SLASH (plans/23): the in-scope counterexample (L8 — a slash REQUIRES a reason, not a bare vote). Read-side gated as a non-empty (non-blank) string; the store is not a sandbox.

freshness.description: broker-signing W1 (plans/35): the freshness-bound-VOUCH replay envelope, IN the body so the frame sig over record_id binds it (Option A). Shape {approved_at:int epoch ms, nonce:string the replay one-shot >= edge-freshness.MIN_NONCE_LEN, key_id:string}. Consumed by the W2 read-gate predicate (edge-freshness.checkFreshnessWindow) -- SHADOW until then. The frame-level 'nonce' (INV-22 identity) is DISTINCT from this freshness nonce (the replay one-shot); W2 reads the freshness one, and DROPS a VOUCH lacking payload.freshness (no-freshness => drop, never skip-when-absent).

config_hash.description: P2 (§1.4): self-asserted config-stability signal (WEAK axis-3); DIRECT keys on (persona, config_hash)

t.description: P2 (§5 dec.6): created_at epoch ms, for exponential decay

idempotency_key.description: optional INV-22 key, re-derivable from the body (record.js deriveIdempotencyKey)

sig.description: optional ed25519 signature over record_id (base64); verified by the frame layer (spec §2)
